From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[Munich](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Munich "wiktionary:Munich")*** or ***[München](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/M%C3%BCnchen "wiktionary:München")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**[Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich")** (German: **München**) is the capital of the German state of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria").

**Munich** or **München** may also refer to:

## Places

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Munich_(disambiguation)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Places")]

* [Munich (district)](/wiki/Munich_(district) "Munich (district)"), adjacent to the capital city
* München, a quarter of [Bad Berka](/wiki/Bad_Berka "Bad Berka"), Thuringia
* München, a quarter of [Hirschbach, Bavaria](/wiki/Hirschbach,_Bavaria "Hirschbach, Bavaria")
* München, a quarter of [Hutthurm](/wiki/Hutthurm "Hutthurm"), Bavaria
* München, a quarter of [Uebigau-Wahrenbrück](/wiki/Uebigau-Wahrenbr%C3%BCck "Uebigau-Wahrenbrück"), Brandenburg
* [Munich, North Dakota](/wiki/Munich,_North_Dakota "Munich, North Dakota"), a city in the United States

## Arts and entertainment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Munich_(disambiguation)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Arts and entertainment")]

* [*Munich* (2005 film)](/wiki/Munich_(2005_film) "Munich (2005 film)"), a Steven Spielberg film based on the 1972 Olympic massacre and its aftermath
* *[Munich – The Edge of War](/wiki/Munich_%E2%80%93_The_Edge_of_War "Munich – The Edge of War")*, a 2021 British drama film based on the novel by Robert Harris
* *Munich*, a 2017 novel by [Robert Harris](/wiki/Robert_Harris_(novelist) "Robert Harris (novelist)")
* ["Munich" (song)](/wiki/Munich_(song) "Munich (song)"), a 2005 song by British rock group Editors
* "Munich", a 2012 song by the Fray from *[Scars & Stories](/wiki/Scars_%26_Stories "Scars & Stories")*
* "Munich", a poem by Patti Smith from her 1978 book *[Babel](/wiki/Babel_(book) "Babel (book)")*

## Ships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Munich_(disambiguation)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ships")]

* [SMS *München*](/wiki/SMS_M%C3%BCnchen "SMS München"), a *Bremen*-class cruiser of the Imperial German Navy
* [MS *München*](/wiki/MS_M%C3%BCnchen "MS München"), a German LASH carrier, lost in a storm in December 1978
* [SS *General von Steuben*](/wiki/SS_General_von_Steuben "SS General von Steuben"), a German luxury passenger ship, renamed from *München* in 1930

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Munich_(disambiguation)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sports")]

* [FC Bayern Munich](/wiki/FC_Bayern_Munich "FC Bayern Munich"), a sports club located in the capital city
* [TSV 1860 Munich](/wiki/TSV_1860_Munich "TSV 1860 Munich"), a sports club also known for its football team
* [Munich (sport shoes)](/wiki/Munich_(sport_shoes) "Munich (sport shoes)"), a Spanish company and brand specialized in sports and fashion shoes

## Other uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Munich_(disambiguation)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other uses")]

* Munich, a common shorthand for [Munich Agreement](/wiki/Munich_Agreement "Munich Agreement"), the 1938 accord ceding the Sudetenland to Germany
* [Munich Group](/wiki/Munich_Group "Munich Group") is a diplomatic initiative in the Middle East
* [Alphonse Munchen](/wiki/Alphonse_Munchen "Alphonse Munchen") (1850–1917), engineer and politician from Luxembourg
* [Munich air disaster](/wiki/Munich_air_disaster "Munich air disaster"), the crash of an aircraft carrying British footballers at Munich-Riem Airport in 1958
* [Munich putsch](/wiki/Beer_Hall_Putsch "Beer Hall Putsch") 1923, also known as Hitler's Beer Hall Putsch
* [Munich massacre](/wiki/Munich_massacre "Munich massacre"), a 1972 Palestine Liberation Organization terrorist attack against Israeli athletes at the Olympics
* [Munich Security Conference](/wiki/Munich_Security_Conference "Munich Security Conference"), an international political forum
* [Munich Mouser](/wiki/Munich_Mouser "Munich Mouser"), a cat
